w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Blue Ridge Automation documents must reflect the company's reputation for precision, clarity, and professionalism. This style guide defines every formatting rule needed to produce consistent Word documents — from proposal decks to technical specifications to service reports.
structured, confident, and easy to navigate
. When in doubt, favor clarity over decoration.
Use only these approved colors in all Blue Ridge documents. Never introduce custom colors or gradients.
Cover bars, document title, footer rules
H1 underline accent, critical callouts only
Alternating table rows, cover meta panel
exclusively. These fonts are pre-installed on all Windows machines and render consistently across all Microsoft Office versions.
The following demonstrates how headings should appear in a real document.
This is body text that follows
1. It uses the BR Body style: Calibri 11
spacing, 6pt after paragraph. Begin every major section with a Heading 1.
Heading 2 is used for sub-sections within a major section. It uses Calibri Bold 14pt in BR Blue Medium. No decorative rule is applied.
Heading 3 marks detailed sub-topics or field labels within a sub-section. Use sparingly. Calibri Bold 12pt in Gray Dark.
Use callout boxes to highlight key facts, important requirements, or critical notes. Do not overuse — maximum one per page section.
IMPORTANT: All control panel designs must conform to UL508A standards before installation. Contact the Project Engineer before deviation from approved drawings.
The callout above uses the BR Callout style: Bold Calibri 11pt, BR Navy text, BR Blue
Use the BR Body Bullet style for unordered lists:
PLC/PAC programming scoped and approved by Lead Engineer
SCADA/HMI development reviewed with client before
Nested sub-bullet for additional detail at level 2
UL508A panel certification completed prior to shipment
Use numbered lists for sequential steps or procedures:
Client shares challenges with the Blue Ridge team
Blue Ridge visits the facility for on-site assessment
Custom solution is designed, implemented, and handed off
Tables use BR Blue header rows with white bold text, thin #CCCCCC borders on all cells, and optional alternating gray rows for readability.
Table 7.1 — Sample Device Register. Alternate rows use BR Gray
Single column (default). Two-column only for reference tables.
Next Page break before each major section when > 3 pages
shows the Blue Ridge company label on the left and the document title on the right in Gray Mid. A thin BR Blue Medium rule runs along the header bottom.
shows 'Confidential — Blue Ridge Automation' on the left and 'Page X of Y' right-aligned. Separated by a thin BR Navy rule. Footer text uses BR Footer style (9pt Gray Mid).
uses a different first-page header/footer — it shows no running header, only the confidentiality line and contact info at the bottom.
Blue Ridge documents communicate with the voice of expert advisors — knowledgeable, direct, and client-focused. Follow these rules:
Write in plain, active voice. Avoid passive constructions where possible.
Capitalize proper system names: Ignition, Allen-Bradley, ISA-88, OEE.
Use Oxford commas in all lists.
Spell out abbreviations on first use: OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness).
Date format: Month DD, YYYY — e.g., March 31, 2026.
Version format: v1.0, v1.1, v2.0 — increment minor for revisions, major for reissues.
Company name in documents: Blue Ridge Automation on first reference, Blue Ridge thereafter.
Use this cheat sheet when starting any new Blue Ridge document.
BR Doc Title (or cover-page large text)
